title: Introduction to Language Studies
outline: This course provides an introduction to the main branches of Linguistics. On completion of the course students would have covered topics such as: phonetics (the production and classification of speech sounds, the International Phonetic Alphabet, suprasegme ntals); phonology (how speech sounds are organised in language; the phoneme -allophone distinction); morphology (form –meaning pairs; morphemes and word -formation; syntax (the rules that govern the phrase, structures of language; elements of generative gramm ar); and semantics relations, semantic features; an introduction to psychological and neurobiological aspects of how humans acquire language.
assessment: Continuous assessment (essays, projects, tests, etc.) counts 100%.
